text:
Marissa Cole: trying to close the loop on cost model v1 scope for Q1. Console needs consistent breakdowns, Optimize needs what-if. can we align on MUST-have fields + endpoints for v1 vs v2?

Talia Benson: +1. I’d like a crisp list we can paste into PRD + release checklist. Also want to avoid "v1 is everything".

Aditya Rao: from Optimize side: we can work with a constrained what-if as long as the inputs are stable and we can compute ROI without hand-wavy assumptions. today we’re blocked by missing/ambiguous cache + quant knobs.

Chloe Martin: Console needs a deterministic breakdown that matches what we show in dashboards (prefill vs decode + overhead). Also we need bounded payload sizes for hot paths.

Jada Williams: + caching strategy matters. if the endpoint is called per-page load and returns 1000 rows of component detail we’re dead.

Ethan Park: ok, proposing we define v1 around 3...

text:
Marissa Cole: trying to lock down 2 things for the console onboarding nudges experiment:
1) placements (where these show)
2) what we count as a “completed” step for measurement (vs just click)

Proposed steps: Create API key → First request → First 100k tokens → View usage dashboard. Would love quick alignment so I can update the spec + send to eng.

Kenji Nakamura: +1. For placement, I’m thinking we keep it super contextual. “Create API key” nudge on the empty state / first-time home + also on the API Keys page header. “First request” on the API Keys success screen + in the quickstart block. “Usage dashboard” on the first request success toast / banner.

Paula Mendes: on measurement: please pls don’t define completion as “clicked CTA” 🙏 we should measure completion as the actual milestone event firing (key_created, request_succeeded, etc). Click is still useful as CTR leading indica...

title: #product: 1768040207
preview:
Marissa Cole: Question for launch decision: do we allow *quality signals* from shadow experiments to trigger **automatic rollback** of a rollout/canary at v1, or advisory-only w/ human in the loop? This is coming up in beta calls. 

Paula Mendes: I’d strongly prefer advisory-only for launch. The rollout engine today...
text:
Marissa Cole: Question for launch decision: do we allow *quality signals* from shadow experiments to trigger **automatic rollback** of a rollout/canary at v1, or advisory-only w/ human in the loop? This is coming up in beta calls. 

Paula Mendes: I’d strongly prefer advisory-only for launch. The rollout engine today auto-rollbacks on hard health checks (5xx, latency) w/ super clear semantics. Quality is probabilistic + can lag.

Dylan Brooks: +1. UX-wise we can do “Regression detected” banner + suggested actions + one-click rollback. But making it automatic seems like we’ll get paged for false positives.

Selene Huang: The biggest issue is statistical stability. Even with deterministic bucketing, you can get noisy deltas on small sample sizes or skewed traffic. If we auto-rollback we need hard requirements: min n, CI bounds, and cool-down.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE POV: please no auto...

text:
Ethan Park: Quick sync - sales flagged 3 paying customers asking for fixes they say block adoption. Do we treat these as hotfix or next minor?
Marta Gomez: Context from sales: two customers (Enterprise A, BetaStart) report model truncation in their ingestion flow; one (Enterprise B) wants a new filtering dropdown (feature).
Priya Kapoor: Rule of thumb: if it blocks customer onboarding or breaks billing/ingest -> hotfix. UI preferences -> next wave unless high-severity for new users.
Alex Morgan: Technical check: truncation looks like tokenization mismatch introduced in last deploy. If root cause is config we can patch in <6 hours. That feels like hotfix material.
Tina Huang: QA note: if it's config-only we can run a quick regression suite. But if it touches tokenization library we'll need 24h for test matrix.
SalesOps-bot: Sales ticket links: #1234, #1256, #1280. Priority: Enterprise ...

title: #product: 1765554321
preview:
Alex Chen: Quick proposal — for Q3 OKRs I want to consolidate embeddings + rerank into a single 'Retrieval' slice to reduce shipping overhead. Key asks: one rollout, unified telemetry, shared caching. Acceptance criteria I’m proposing:
1) end-to-end p95 latency < 250ms for typical 1k-token rerank workloads

text:
Alex Chen: Quick proposal — for Q3 OKRs I want to consolidate embeddings + rerank into a single 'Retrieval' slice to reduce shipping overhead. Key asks: one rollout, unified telemetry, shared caching. Acceptance criteria I’m proposing:
1) end-to-end p95 latency < 250ms for typical 1k-token rerank workloads
2) embeddings 99th < 60ms for small-vector calls
3) regression delta < 1% on our 10 seed customer prompt sets
4) canary + instant fallback to previous model/version.
Thoughts? tagging @eng-runtime @sales

Maya Patel: I like consolidation for developer velocity, but worried about a cost spike for high-throughput rerank. We should bake in cost guardrails: autoscale ceilings + a soft-degrade (approx-match fallback) when cost thresholds hit.

Samir Rao: +1 to cost guards. Also need per-route batching hints and a required quantization plan. Suggest we require a quant profile and a benchm...

title: #product: 1763652104
preview:
Aditya Rao: Need a quick gut check on MVP scope for Optimize quality gates before I lock PRD copy. Two open q’s:
1) which config changes must run FULL gates vs FAST smoke gates?
2) how should overrides look in Console (and what do we allow at MVP)?

Paula Mendes: +1. Also from Rollouts side we need a crisp rule we c...
text:
Aditya Rao: Need a quick gut check on MVP scope for Optimize quality gates before I lock PRD copy. Two open q’s:
1) which config changes must run FULL gates vs FAST smoke gates?
2) how should overrides look in Console (and what do we allow at MVP)?

Paula Mendes: +1. Also from Rollouts side we need a crisp rule we can encode (block canary promotion unless gate status == passed or overridden).

Marissa Cole: Let’s anchor on “safe-by-default” but don’t accidentally make Optimize unusable for low-risk changes.

Jonas Weber: From evals POV: batching + cache settings usually change latency/throughput, but can still change outputs in edge cases (timeouts, truncation, different batching order, etc). Quantization is *def* higher risk.

Selene Huang: Agree. We’ve seen quality deltas on quant profile switches, especially long context. I’d vote: quant profile changes => always full gate.

Aditya...

Marta Ruiz: Kicking off a short thread to capture decisions from the routing dials deep-dive. Goal: crisp launch criteria + customer promise for multi-region + tenant isolation. Keep replies short, add owners.
Liam O'Connor: Thinking in 'dials' helps: latency-first, cost-first, residency-first. Each dial maps to a policy template and a cost estimate.
Priya Nair: Implementation note: templates translate to routing rules + scheduler hints. We need explicit model variant mapping (quantized vs fp16) per template.
Ethan Park: SRE concern: how do we surface capacity headroom per-region? Need a headroom metric and a hard-fail path. Also define evacuation behavior when headroom < 5%.
Sofia Gomez: Sales feedback: customers want two things: predictable pricing line item for "isolation" and a one-liner SLA we can show prospects. We need an agreed phrase and price bucket.

text:
Talia Benson: hey folks — quick alignment request for the status page transparency upgrades. before we lock the launch plan, can we agree on (a) launch criteria and (b) measurable outcomes? want something concrete for PM-620 + the internal announcement.

Marissa Cole: +1. also helpful for Console integration timing — we need a crisp “what ships in phase 1 vs phase 2” so we don’t block on UX polish.

Paula Mendes: agree. comms/templates are getting close, but “done” keeps moving because we don’t have explicit gates.

Rafael Mendes: ok, propose we define criteria per rollout phase:
- Phase 0 (internal dogfood): taxonomy + mapping in place, incident-bot drafting working, and on-call can actually use it without extra steps
- Phase 1 (limited public rollout): components visible + templates live + manual posting workflow solid
- Phase 2 (full): automation + audits + post-incident summaries ...

Ava Chen: Heads-up — planning to expose the new "Insights (beta)" panels in Console behind a hidden flag. Need a quick plan for canary audiences + guardrails.

Ava Chen: flag key = console.beta.insights_ui. We want role-based first — product and analytics teams + 5% of DAU orgs as canary. then expand to 25% if no regressions.

title: #product: 1748761155
preview:
Lauren Bishop: trying to close semantics for “predictive mode” vs reactive scaling before we freeze PRD copy + Console strings. Can we align on precedence + what happens when forecast disagrees w/ saturation?

Lauren Bishop: specifically:
1) Is predictive “an extra signal” or “primary desired, with reactive override...
text:
Lauren Bishop: trying to close semantics for “predictive mode” vs reactive scaling before we freeze PRD copy + Console strings. Can we align on precedence + what happens when forecast disagrees w/ saturation?

Lauren Bishop: specifically:
1) Is predictive “an extra signal” or “primary desired, with reactive overrides”?
2) When reactive says scale up NOW, do we ignore forecast?
3) How do we log/communicate “why” to customers (auditability)?

Vanessa Ortiz: my preference: treat forecast as a *bias* / floor on desired (ahead of time), but reactive is still the safety net.
Vanessa Ortiz: in other words: desired = max(reactive_desired, predictive_desired) for scale-up path, then clamp to bounds. For scale-down, be more conservative.

Keiko Yamamoto: +1 on max() for scale up. For scale down: min(reactive, predictive) is risky if predictor is wrong / missing.
Keiko Yamamoto: we should probab...

Ava Chen: Nice. Confirming runtime-v2 canary -> yes, ran smoke for payments + embeddings yesterday :white_check_mark:. Latency is within target on staged traffic.

Ravi Patel: For quant-tuning, estimate is ~2 more sprints. Main blocker is the model accuracy regressions on long contexts. I'm pairing with Priya this week to tighten eval sets. :hourglass_flowing_sand:

Priya Singh: ETA on regressions: I'll finish the baseline evals by Thu and open a triage doc. If anyone has recent failing prompts, drop them here.

title: #product: 1748129999
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick design question for the compact Autoscale row: should min/max steppers compress into a single control on narrow widths? Sketch: https://www.figma.com/file/xyz/compact-autoscale
Diego Rivera: If we compress, we must preserve a11y labels for each value. Two hidden labels isn't ideal. Maybe a single dua...
text:
Ava Chen: Quick design question for the compact Autoscale row: should min/max steppers compress into a single control on narrow widths? Sketch: https://www.figma.com/file/xyz/compact-autoscale
Diego Rivera: If we compress, we must preserve a11y labels for each value. Two hidden labels isn't ideal. Maybe a single dual-thumb input? :thinking:
Sam Ortiz: Dual-thumb is neat but might be unfamiliar. Another option: collapsed mode shows `Min 1 | Max 8` and a chevron to expand to steppers. Keeps visual simplicity.
Priya Sharma: UX note: when collapsed, show a small delta badge that says `+2 burst` if burst is enabled. Users scan that faster than numbers. Also include an affordance to edit inline with keyboard (Enter opens steppers).
Ava Chen: Good call. Accessibility: how do we announce the collapse state to screen readers? Suggest `aria-expanded` on the chevron and an offscreen summary: `Au...

title: #product: 1747532100
preview:
Ava Chen: quick design ping — new autoscale mock with a few novel bits: 'min floor' concept, a separate 'burst cap' slider, and a hold-to-apply on the SLO slider to reduce accidental high commits. want copy + accessibility eyes.
Noah Kim: Interesting. "Min floor" language could be confusing with existing "min" field...
text:
Ava Chen: quick design ping — new autoscale mock with a few novel bits: 'min floor' concept, a separate 'burst cap' slider, and a hold-to-apply on the SLO slider to reduce accidental high commits. want copy + accessibility eyes.
Noah Kim: Interesting. "Min floor" language could be confusing with existing "min" field. Maybe show it as "Min (guaranteed baseline)" and keep shorthand label.
Priya Shah: UX concern: hold-to-apply might be annoying for power users. Maybe show a confirmation pill only if change > 20% from current value.
Sofi Gomez: a11y: NVDA reads the new "burst cap" slider as just "slider" unless we add aria-label and aria-valuetext. Also ensure the hold-to-apply action is keyboard activatable (Enter after focus) and that assistive tech hears the pending state.
Marco Rivera: Copy idea for burst helper: "Burst cap: maximum temporary GPUs allowed during spikes. Not permanent;...

preview:

Allison Grant: +1. We should define it in terms of (a) shipped artifacts, (b...
text:
Vivek Kulkarni: Need quick alignment here so we can lock messaging + scope. What do we mean by “supported” for Private observability minimum pack vs “premium”? Right now docs are fuzzy and Support keeps asking what they’re on the hook for.

Allison Grant: +1. We should define it in terms of (a) shipped artifacts, (b) default config we test/validate, (c) what Support/SRE will troubleshoot vs best-effort.

Neha Kapoor: From SRE POV: “supported” should mean if customer installs with our Helm values (or minimally modified) we can rely on dashboards + alerts being accurate and not exploding their Prometheus. If they swap out Prometheus/Grafana entirely (Datadog, etc) we can’t guarantee.

Priya Natarajan: Agree. We need crisp: supported == validated in reference envs + upgrade safe + documented runbooks. Anything else is “integrates with” not “supported”.

Logan Wright: For telemetry stack ...

title: #product: 1746210905

Ben Carter: +1. We need signals that correlate w/ “would t...
text:
Nikhil Sharma: Trying to lock “intent signals v1” today so Logan can finish the schema + we can stop bikeshedding in PRD. What *counts* as intent (docs views / pricing clicks / security page views) vs vanity? Need something we can defend to Sales + Privacy.

Ben Carter: +1. We need signals that correlate w/ “would talk to a human about Dedicated/Private” not “someone Googled us”.

Elliot Price: From docs side: we can fire events on doc views w/ URL + referrer + time-on-page bucket + “cta clicked” (e.g., “Contact sales”, “Request VPC”). But we should decide what’s meaningful.

Tyler Benson: Also need to be careful about marketing touch conflict. If we treat every pricing page view as intent, we’ll spam folks who are just comparing.

Camila Reyes: + privacy note: avoid passing full URLs w/ query params into HubSpot notes. We can map to a doc_id / page_category and keep raw in analytics ...

text:
Alex Chen: Heads-up 1 - our DP private preview funnel is underperforming. 50 sent, 3 completed onboarding. We need quick fixes.
Maria Gomez: Saw that. A few people reported the invite form was too long and asked for billing details up front. That likely kills conversion.
Jordan Blake: Also the onboarding checklist references an old role name for service accounts. Confusing and blocks step 3.
Samir Patel: And corporate mail filters are dropping the slack invite link for some domains. Ill add an email fallback with a one-click token.
Alex Chen: Can we split into two outreach paths: fast try (very light touch) vs design partner (commitment asks)?
Maria Gomez: Yes. Fast path = no org questions, single CTA, basic setup doc. DP path = commitment form, NDA, 4-week feedback cadence.
Jordan Blake: Suggest for DP ask we specify per-feature asks instead of vague "feedback": e.g., quality eval on...

text:
Ava Chen: kicking off intake for a scoped PRD — 'express path' for high-priority enterprise tenants. problem: some customers need sub-100ms token latency for critical flows and are willing to trade budget for predictable SLOs. want to capture goals, non-goals, constraints, and launch metrics here.

Ava Chen: dedicated-first. we can discuss hosted later as a stretch. non-goal: no changes to public API surface.
Priya Singh: high-level goals: 1) guarantee p50 < 80ms p95 < 150ms for premium tenants under normal load, 2) provide cost-cap knob per-tenant, 3) rollout controls (canary + instant rollback).

Henry Cho: +1. Goal is “clear + actionable” without scaring folks. Also want consistency between docs, support macros, and what status page says.

Christine Wong: From eng side: canary is *only* enabling the new early-shed paths + consistent Retry-After. We *will* return more explicit overload responses instead of timeouts. So customers may notice 503s where previously they saw hangs.

title: #product: 1741596602
preview:
Marissa Cole: need quick alignment on pricing/packaging + messaging for Quantization Profile Library before next beta update. Q: is this “included in Optimize” (paid) or available to all (opt-in) and Optimize is just the tooling/evals? also wording: “library” vs “recommendation”.

Paula Mendes: +1. telemetry side we...
text:
Marissa Cole: need quick alignment on pricing/packaging + messaging for Quantization Profile Library before next beta update. Q: is this “included in Optimize” (paid) or available to all (opt-in) and Optimize is just the tooling/evals? also wording: “library” vs “recommendation”.

Paula Mendes: +1. telemetry side we can expose impact panels (cost/token deltas, fallback_rate, etc) either way, but packaging impacts what we promise. If we say Optimize includes it, ppl will expect “turn it on and savings happen” + recommendations.

Miguel Santos: Sales POV: if it’s included in Optimize, it’s a clean upsell story (“use Optimize to cut costs”). but enterprise will ask: is the quantization itself gated, or just dashboards/support? we should avoid surprise paywall during beta.

Markus Klein: design partner feedback from Acme AI + Northstar: they like that it’s explicit per-route, but they’re ...

title: #product: 1741104321
preview:
Priya Rao: Quick proposal: add ephemeral/session-scoped saved queries to Console analytics. Server-side TTL (default 24h), sharable with short-lived tokens. Use case: temporary filter experiments without cluttering org library.

text:
Priya Rao: Quick proposal: add ephemeral/session-scoped saved queries to Console analytics. Server-side TTL (default 24h), sharable with short-lived tokens. Use case: temporary filter experiments without cluttering org library.
Ben Carter: Agreed. API change: add scope enum (session|route|org) and ttl_seconds. Include a convert_to_org endpoint.
Asha Kumar: UX: show an "ephemeral" pill with relative countdown (e.g., "23h left"). Hover shows exact expiry. Sort persistent saves above ephemeral ones.
Priya Rao: Sharing: signed link tokens that expire. If token used after expiry return 410.
Ben Carter: Telemetry: emit events with fields saved_query.id, scope, created_by, expires_at, attached_routes. When executed include model and token_count_estimate. Executions billed against route/model quotas.
Asha Kumar: Guardrails: block attaching session-scoped queries to alerts/exports; require con...

title: #product: 1740779002

Henry Cho: +1. Also needs to be something we can measure reliably from telemetry (not “user felt happy”).

text:
Nikhil Sharma: We need to lock a definition for “time-to-first-success” (TTFS) for Quickstart Templates v1. Otherwise launch KPI dashboard is going to be bikeshedding forever. What’s the *first success*?

Henry Cho: +1. Also needs to be something we can measure reliably from telemetry (not “user felt happy”).

Talia Benson: Can we list candidate definitions + what events we can actually emit? Then pick one for v1 + a stretch metric.

Monica Patel: From DevEx POV “success” should map to the user seeing something *work* locally: they scaffold template, set env vars, run, get a response. If we make it “deploy to prod” we’ll miss most PLG users.

Elliot Price: Agree. Docs framing is “get to first working request” then “deploy + observe” as next steps. TTFS should align with the first milestone.

Wei Chen: Users in interviews basically said “if I can ask one question and it returns an answ...

title: #product: 1740579013
preview:
Naomi Feldman: want to lock MVP scope for the “baseline compliance reporting pack” this week. Goal = unblock enterprise security reviews (Northbridge/Helio), not build a full GRC product. What are the *must-haves* vs can-wait?

Olga Petrov: from GRC POV, the minimum pack that passes most SOC2/ISO-ish questionnaires:...
text:
Naomi Feldman: want to lock MVP scope for the “baseline compliance reporting pack” this week. Goal = unblock enterprise security reviews (Northbridge/Helio), not build a full GRC product. What are the *must-haves* vs can-wait?

Olga Petrov: from GRC POV, the minimum pack that passes most SOC2/ISO-ish questionnaires:
1) retention config export (by data class) + change history summary
2) audit log coverage summary (what events we emit + where they’re stored) + time-windowed export
3) data residency/region summary (where customer data/logs live)
4) access control / RBAC report (roles, permission model, who has admin)
5) evidence checklist w/ “how generated” + owner + frequency

Olga Petrov: Anything less and we’ll still be doing bespoke responses every deal.

Marcus Lin: +1. Also need clear language on “what’s NOT included” (e.g., we don’t store prompts/completions unless customer enable...

text:
Aditya Rao: want to lock recommendations MVP scope today. goal: something we can show internally + a couple friendly customers w/ CS, but *no* fancy UX.

Paula Mendes: +1. we need to define “actionable” so we don’t ship a bunch of vague “try caching more” statements.

Chloe Martin: console stub wise, I can display a table with: route, model, rec text, impact estimate, confidence, and “how to apply” snippet. but we need a stable schema.

Felix Schneider: and filters. at least: deployment mode, region, model, route. anything more is future.

Logan Wright: reminder: telemetry join keys are still being finalized. route_id + model_version_id should be stable, tenant_id is a non-starter for MVP (cardinality + privacy).

title: #product: 1740201122

Marissa Cole: We need to lock GA scope for Hosted canary rollout workflow this week. Beta feedback is good but we’re creeping. Two big asks surfacing: (1) automated quality gates as hard stop, (2) Dedicated/Private parity at GA. We can’t do everything. Let’s decide.

Paula Mendes: +1. For GA I’m pushing for policy g...
text:
Marissa Cole: We need to lock GA scope for Hosted canary rollout workflow this week. Beta feedback is good but we’re creeping. Two big asks surfacing: (1) automated quality gates as hard stop, (2) Dedicated/Private parity at GA. We can’t do everything. Let’s decide.

Paula Mendes: +1. For GA I’m pushing for policy guardrails + telemetry auto-stop/rollback to be non-negotiable. Quality gates as hard gate feels risky/immature unless we standardize signals + avoid false positives.

Dylan Brooks: Eng POV: Console wizard + timeline + activity log are on track if we don’t add a bunch of new gating UI. If quality gates become “required step” we have to ship: config UX, storage schema, eval signal ingestion, and a bunch of error states. That’s easily another 3–5 weeks and introduces new failure modes.

Mina Haddad: Data plane side: Hosted split enforcement is basically ready (region-aware det...

title: #product: 1739560067
preview:

Tessa Morgan: +1. For UX I need a definitive “MVP workflows” list. Right now wi...
text:
Naomi Feldman: Need quick alignment on Console RBAC v2 role management MVP scope for the enterprise pilots. What *must* be in v1 of the UI vs what can stay API-only initially. Goal is to unblock PM-661 + DES-914 and keep eng scope sane.

Tessa Morgan: +1. For UX I need a definitive “MVP workflows” list. Right now wire notes assume: browse roles/templates, view permissions, assign roles. But “custom roles” vs “templates-only” is still open.

Chloe Martin: From console backend POV, we can ship a clean MVP if we keep it to:
- role list + role detail (incl permissions)
- role binding CRUD for users (and maybe groups)
- template picker for new roles (optional)
Custom role editor (adding/removing individual permissions) is a big surface + lots of edge cases.

Marcus Lin: Security vote: MVP must support least privilege *in practice*. If we ship only 4 templates and no ability to trim permiss...

title: #product: 1739553314
preview:
Marissa Cole: want to lock beta vs GA scope for Quantization Profile Library this week so we can stop thrashing on tickets. pls reply w/ concerns + what you need for launch criteria.

Marissa Cole: my current proposal:
- Beta: allowlist + 2–3 design partners, hosted + 1 dedicated, route-level opt-in only, profiles p...
text:
Marissa Cole: want to lock beta vs GA scope for Quantization Profile Library this week so we can stop thrashing on tickets. pls reply w/ concerns + what you need for launch criteria.

Marissa Cole: my current proposal:
- Beta: allowlist + 2–3 design partners, hosted + 1 dedicated, route-level opt-in only, profiles published for 2–3 model variants max, manual profile selection (explicit version), kill-switch + rollback runbook required, dashboards required.
- GA: self-serve enablement for all tenants (still opt-in per route), broader model coverage + “recommended” alias, hardened compatibility matrix + clear error messaging, docs + support playbook.

Talia Benson: +1 on freezing this. Can we define “beta exit” vs “GA entry” gates explicitly? otherwise we’ll keep getting drive-bys from eng leadership.

Paula Mendes: from rollout/telemetry side: beta *must* have:
- per-route enabled_rate...

title: #product: 1738789012
preview:
Marissa Cole: Need decision today-ish: force-promote policy for Dedicated staged rollouts. When allowed + what does customer admin see in Console/audit logs?

Lauren Bishop: +1. Customers are asking for "escape hatch" but if we expose it too freely it defeats gates. Dedicated folks are also very change-managed.

Pau...
text:
Marissa Cole: Need decision today-ish: force-promote policy for Dedicated staged rollouts. When allowed + what does customer admin see in Console/audit logs?

Lauren Bishop: +1. Customers are asking for "escape hatch" but if we expose it too freely it defeats gates. Dedicated folks are also very change-managed.

Paula Mendes: framing: we have 3 actions that bypass automation: force-promote, force-rollback/abort, hold. Hold is already kinda benign (stops progression). Force-promote is the spicy one.

Kenji Nakamura: UX-wise: if force-promote exists and customers can click it, we need scary confirmations + "why" and show that it bypassed gates. If only internal, we can keep customer UI simpler.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: from security/compliance: if any override exists, it must be audit logged. Question is: which audit log (customer-visible vs internal) and whether customer can perform it w/ RBAC...

title: #product: 1738700440
preview:
Marissa Cole: Trying to lock MVP defaults for Dedicated staged rollouts. Proposal: default stages = `1% -> 10% -> 50% -> 100%` for traffic split. Question: do we support arbitrary stages (ex: 5/25/75) in v1 or keep it simple?

Lauren Bishop: +1 on 1/10/50/100 as default. Matches what customers ask for ("canary, then...
text:
Marissa Cole: Trying to lock MVP defaults for Dedicated staged rollouts. Proposal: default stages = `1% -> 10% -> 50% -> 100%` for traffic split. Question: do we support arbitrary stages (ex: 5/25/75) in v1 or keep it simple?

Lauren Bishop: +1 on 1/10/50/100 as default. Matches what customers ask for ("canary, then ramp"). I’m worried arbitrary stages becomes: customers create 12 stages, then get stuck + blame us.

Paula Mendes: From gates POV, more stages = more gate evals + more chances for missing/noisy metrics to block. Also harder to standardize thresholds / windows.

Kenji Nakamura: UX concern: if we allow arbitrary stages, we need a full editor (add/remove/reorder, validations, copy changes, warnings). Timeline component gets messy fast. If we keep the timeline to 4 nodes + promote button, it’s legible.

Marissa Cole: For Console: the stage timeline is already tight on smaller...

title: #product: 1738449012
preview:

Soojin Lee: From packaging POV, Standard/Plus/Premium is intuitive, but “Plus” reads a bit consume...
text:
Lauren Bishop: Kicking off naming + positioning for Dedicated tiers. Current draft: Standard / Plus / Premium. Need alignment on what we *promise* around (a) burst and (b) failover. Please chime in w/ strong opinions.

Soojin Lee: From packaging POV, Standard/Plus/Premium is intuitive, but “Plus” reads a bit consumer-y. Alternatives we’ve heard: Standard / Pro / Enterprise, or Core / Plus / Elite.

Stephanie Nguyen: Sales: Standard/Plus/Premium is fine IF it maps cleanly to what we can say in a call + order form. Biggest issue last quarter was AEs saying “you can burst whenever” and then it turned into support tickets.

Isabelle Laurent: +1. Also need discount guardrails per tier, so names should be durable (don’t want to rename in 2 months).

Rachel Kim: For enterprise accounts, “Premium” works, but they will immediately ask: “Premium = multi-region active/active?” If answer is no, w...

title: #product: 1738354821
preview: Lauren Bishop: Hey folks — need to close out open questions on Dedicated throughput SLO definition this week so I can finalize customer-facing language + tier entitlements. Can we align on: (1) canonical metric (tokens/sec vs req/sec, streaming), (2) measurement window, (3) exclusions (customer throttling, model/ver...
text:
Lauren Bishop: Hey folks — need to close out open questions on Dedicated throughput SLO definition this week so I can finalize customer-facing language + tier entitlements. Can we align on: (1) canonical metric (tokens/sec vs req/sec, streaming), (2) measurement window, (3) exclusions (customer throttling, model/version, warmup), and (4) how we report vs what we page on?

Sean Gallagher: +1. SRE side wants one canonical metric for compliance + burn-rate alerting. The main unresolved bit is streaming + batching: do we measure "output tokens" only, or output+prefill? Also: do we normalize by "admitted" requests or all incoming.

Rafael Mendes: For paging: we need something that doesn’t flap from workload mix changes. If the SLO is a throughput *floor*, we need to be careful about customers changing prompt length and then saying we missed.

Lauren Bishop: Right. Product intent: customer ...

text:
Naomi Feldman: trying to lock beta criteria + who we invite. want to get this thread settled today so I can update the launch brief + customer email.

Naomi Feldman: reminders: beta = SIEM-ready audit export (Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs intake, generic S3). must be explicit on limitations: Hosted + Dedicated only, no Private, limited backfill.

Talia Benson: +1. can you paste current criteria draft here? I can translate into the enablement checklist + rollout plan.

Naomi Feldman: current draft:
1) customer has a real SIEM use case (Splunk or Datadog) OR wants S3 delivery into their pipeline
2) ok w/ beta disclaimer (at-least-once delivery, possible dupes, schema v2 still stabilizing)
3) willing to do weekly check-ins + share feedback + allow us to look at exporter metrics for their tenant
4) volume: not huge (we can set caps), and they can tolerate initial tuning on throttles
5) regions...
